conventions.testing: Unit tests in src files, integration tests in tests/ directory, fixtures in tests/security_fixtures
conventions.linting: ruff for python, biome for js/ts, cargo clippy/fmt for rust
conventions.error_handling: anyhow for applications, thiserror for libraries

patterns: LLM-based validation to reduce false positives

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents the gap tree-sitter aims to fill

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Merged PR implementing the limitation test

constraints: Must maintain performance for large diffs
Should integrate with existing Finding and Severity types
test_coverage: Comprehensive coverage for regex patterns; includes explicit negative tests for multi-line vulnerabilities that currently fail to be detected.

library_findings.notes: Core library for parsing and querying ASTs
Language-specific grammar for Rust AST analysis

approaches.name: Hybrid Regex-AST Scanner
approaches.description: Keep PatternEngine for simple secrets/patterns, but add an AstScanner that uses tree-sitter queries for complex multi-line vulnerabilities like SQLi or Command Injection.

approaches.name: Tree-sitter Query Engine
approaches.description: Implement a generic AstPatternEngine that loads S-expression queries from JSON/files and executes them across the entire file AST instead of line-by-line.

approaches.name: AST-Enhanced LLM Validation
approaches.description: Use tree-sitter to extract relevant code blocks (functions/classes) rather than just N lines of context, then send the structured block to the existing SecurityValidator.
cons: Doesn't solve the 'missing detection' problem, only the 'false positive' problem

recommendation: Hybrid Regex-AST Scanner. It allows for incremental adoption of tree-sitter for the most problematic multi-line vulnerabilities (SQLi, Command Injection) while keeping the fast regex engine for simple patterns like hardcoded secrets.
